emails.text_body:
--Hi Team,

I have a different view on a couple of the recommendations shared today.

For Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL), while the report maintains a
BUY rating with a target price of ₹393, I believe the stock should be rated
SELL with a fair value of around ₹250.

For LTIMindtree (LTM), the report has a REDUCE rating with a target price
of ₹4,115. In my view, the stock deserves an ADD rating, with a target
price of ₹4,075.

Regards,
Yash

Subject: JMFS Fundamental Research - Daily Financial Market Digest (24th April 2026)

Dear All,

JMFS Research
April 24th, 2026
Daily Financial Market Digest

Top Corporate News & sector developments

Lux Industries: Company's board has given in-principle approval to demerge the business into three distinct verticals (with Verticals A and C being listed separately) while the PKT family retains overall control following a family settlement that also revises brand licensing pacts. Positive

Inventurus Knowledge Solutions: The company has announced a major cross-border acquisition, signing a deal to buy US-based healthcare IT firm TruBridge for up to $565 million. Positive

Himadri Speciality Chemical: The company has successfully commenced operations at its new anode material production facility in West Bengal, which boasts an installed capacity of 200 MTPA. Positive

Gujarat Themis Biosyn: Company has signed an Asset Purchase Agreement with Sanofi to acquire 13 established branded generic products for €158 million in cash, expected to close by December 2026. The portfolio, present in 55+ countries across Europe, the Middle East and Africa, reported net sales of ~€62 million in 2025. It strengthens GTBL's global generics platform, expands its anti-infective presence and enables forward integration leveraging its existing API and fermentation capabilities.

Eternal: As per media reports Zomato has agreed to drop a contract term that penalized restaurants for offering cheaper meals to walk-in diners, after opposition from eateries who said the policy undermined their pricing decisions. The clause was never enforced but has been dropped now.

Force Motors: The automaker has executed an agreement to acquire Veera Tanneries for Rs 162 crore, turning it into a wholly-owned subsidiary. Positive

Waaree energies & Vikram Solar: The US Commerce Department has imposed preliminary anti-dumping duties on solar imports from India (123%), Laos (22.46%), and Indonesia (35.17%), following a petition by American solar manufacturers alleging unfair pricing. Combined with existing countervailing duties, total AD/CVD rates reach ~234% for India. Negative

GMR Airports: ADP (Paris airport operator) plans to sell up to a 7.3% stake in GMR Airports for ~€924 million ($1.08 billion). The deal begins with an immediate offload of a 3.4% stake for €256 million (~₹2,800 crore), with the remainder to follow.
4QFY26 Business Update

United Breweries: Heineken India saw net revenue grow low-single-digit. Total volume (including contract brewing now classified as licensed volume) grew mid-single-digit. Premium segment grew mid-teens, led by Kingfisher Ultra. Launched Kingfisher Smooth in mainstream segment.

Gujarat Gas: The company ensured an uninterrupted gas supply to the Morbi unit to support the revival of the local ceramic industry, providing price stability amid ongoing geopolitical disruptions that previously caused a surge in raw material costs. Gas consumption in Morbi has rebounded to ~2.70 Mmscmd in April after operations of several ceramic units were suspended in March, which had temporarily affected the livelihood of nearly 2 lakh workers.

Aditya Birla Real Estate (FY26): Birla Estates reported booking value of Rs 8,136 crore. Collections increased 23.5% year-on-year.
4QFY26 Result Update

Tata Capital: Company reported a healthy quarter with PAT growth at +47% YoY/+16% QoQ (despite missing estimate by 4.7%), and ~17bps/49bps increase in RoA/RoE at 2.1%/13%. This was driven by healthy AUM growth, improving asset quality and continued operating leverage. AUM grew +20% YoY / +6% QoQ (28% YoY ex-TMFL), in-line with their guidance, led by sustained traction in retail, corporate and housing finance. TCHFL remained a standout with +29% YoY AUM growth and best-in-class asset quality. However, at the overall level, NIM performance remained relatively softer than expected (-9bps QoQ, -15bps JMFe) as asset-side repricing and higher corporate mix continued to weigh on yields, leading to a miss in our PAT estimates. Credit cost improved sharply to 0.9% (0.8% ex-motor finance), while GS3/NS3 declined by 20bps/15bps QoQ. Strong Results

Infosys: Company reported 4QFY26 results – revenue and margins were below expectations. Revenues came in at USD 5,040mn, -1.2% QoQ/+6.6% YoY (estimates: USD 5,101mn). Revenues declined 1.3% cc QoQ vs estimates of -0.5% QoQ.  EBIT margin came in at 20.9%, down ~20bps QoQ, vs estimates of 21.2%. Margin tailwinds: currency (+40bps), realisations (+30bps), Margin headwinds: amortization impact (-50bps), one-off gain normalization (-30bps), employee costs (-20 bps). Reported PAT stood at Rs 85.1bn, versus JMFL estimates of Rs 77.7bn, PAT was supported by reversal of tax of Rs 7.7bn. Infy adr was down 4%. Weak Results

LTM: Company reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were below versus expectations. Revenues came in at USD 1,222mn, +1.2% QoQ/+8.1% YoY (estimates: USD 1,227mn). Revenues grew 1.2% cc QoQ vs estimates of 1.6% QoQ. EBIT margin came in at 15.1%, down 100bps QoQ/ up 130bps YoY, vs estimates of 15.6%. This was largely due to partial wage hikes and productivity commitments in key accounts, partially offset by FX gains. Reported PAT stood at Rs 13.9bn, versus estimates of Rs 14.8bn. PAT was positively impacted by reversal of Rs 622mn in labour code impact. Weak Results

Cyient: Company reported mixed Q4 results, with revenue increasing 4.2% QoQ to ₹1,927 crore from ₹1,849 crore. However, profitability weakened as EBIT declined 6.8% QoQ to ₹156 crore from ₹167 crore, with EBIT margin contracting by 95 basis points to 8.08% from 9.03%. Net profit saw a sharp drop of 40.3% QoQ, falling to ₹54.8 crore compared to ₹91.8 crore in the previous quarter.

CIE Automotive India: Company reported a strong performance in Q4FY26, with revenue rising to Rs 2611.9 crore from Rs 2272.6 crore, marking a 14.9% YoY and 9.1% QoQ increase. EBITDA grew to Rs 401.9 crore from Rs 335.5 crore, up 19.8% YoY and 20.1% QoQ, while EBITDA margin improved to 15.4% compared to 14.7% YoY and 13.9% QoQ. Profit after tax also saw solid growth, reaching Rs 249.3 crore versus Rs 206.4 crore, reflecting a 20.8% YoY and 22.0% QoQ increase.
Corporate Action
Cyient Ltd: The company has announced a share buyback worth Rs 720 crore. The buyback will be executed at a fixed price of Rs 1,125 per share, which reflects a premium of nearly 20 per cent over the stock’s closing price. The company will carry out the entire process through the tender offer route
Rolex Rings: The company's Board has approved a buyback of up to 1 crore equity shares at ₹180 per share, totalling ₹180 crores. The shares, with a face value of ₹1 each, represent 3.67% of the existing paid-up equity capital. The buyback will be conducted via the tender offer route, open to all equity shareholders on a proportionate basis as of a record date to be announced.

Company/Sector Update

Infosys | Soft 4QFY26; Healthy large deal wins<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21219&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 1,500

Infosys reported 4QFY26 results – revenue and margins were below expectations. Revenue declined 1.3% cc QoQ vs expectation of 0.5% cc QoQ decline. EBIT margins at 20.9% – missed JMFL and consensus expectations. Forward looking indicators – i) Infosys guided for 1.5%-3.5% YoY cc revenue growth for FY27 (includes 20-25bps of acquisition), lower than our expectation of 2% to 4%, margin guidance was in-line at 20-22%. ii) LTM large deal TCV grew ~28% YoY in FY26 versus decline of ~34% in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 0.74x in FY26 versus 0.60x in FY25). ii) Headcount declined 2.6% QoQ / up 1.6% YoY. iii) Management commentary – Lower end of guidance factors demand deterioration while top end factors demand improvement. Margin headwinds for FY27 also includes a potential 70bps dilutive impact from acquisitions. Prefer Infosys among the top 6 Indian IT companies given healthy large deal wins. Valuations are at ~16x FY27 consensus EPS. We revise our EPS estimates down by 1-2% over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 1,500 (versus INR 1525 earlier). Maintain BUY.

Tata Capital | Strong growth and asset quality trends; miss on NIM<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21220&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 380

Tata Capital reported a healthy quarter with PAT growth at +47% YoY/+16% QoQ (despite missing JMFe by 4.7%), and ~17bps/49bps increase in RoA/RoE at 2.1%/13%. This was driven by healthy AUM growth, improving asset quality and continued operating leverage. AUM grew +20% YoY / +6% QoQ (28% YoY ex-TMFL), in-line with their guidance, led by sustained traction in retail, corporate and housing finance. TCHFL remained a standout with +29% YoY AUM growth and best-in-class asset quality. However, at the overall level, NIM performance remained relatively softer than expected (-9bps QoQ, -15bps JMFe) as asset-side repricing and higher corporate mix continued to weigh on yields, leading to a miss in our PAT estimates. Credit cost improved sharply to 0.9% (0.8% ex-motor finance), while GS3/NS3 declined by 20bps/15bps QoQ. Motor finance also showed improving profitability. Management remains confident of delivering FY28 guidance of ~2.5–2.7% RoA (% of net loans) and >30% PAT CAGR. We believe improving earnings quality and motor finance normalisation support premium valuations. We build in EPS CAGR of ~34% during FY26-28E with average RoA (% of avg. assets)/RoE of ~2.2%/15% in FY27/28E (BAF/CIFC- EPS CAGR: ~28%/26%, RoA: ~4%/2.6%; RoE: ~21%/20% during the same period). We maintain ADD with revised TP of INR380, valuing it at ~2.7/18x FY28 BVPS/EPS (vs. ~3.8/19x for BAF/ ~3.1/18x for CIFC).

LTM | Soft 4QFY26; valuation remains elevated<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21218&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              REDUCE                       INR 4,115

LTM reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were below versus expectations. Revenue growth came in at 1.2% cc QoQ versus expectation of 1.6% cc QoQ. EBIT margins at 15.1% versus JMFL/consensus expectations of 15.6%/15.5%. Forward looking indicators – i) LTM order inflow grew 10.2% YoY in FY26 versus 7% YoY growth in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 1.4x in FY26 versus 1.3x in FY25). ii) Headcount remained stable on a sequential basis (+4.3% YoY). iii) Management commentary – confident to continue the growth momentum in FY27. However, due to certain macroeconomic environment that might happen within the three months period, there could be some softness in a particular quarter. Valuations are at ~21x FY27 consensus EPS. LTM is at ~7% premium to Mphasis for a similar growth profile in FY27, as per consensus. We reduce our EPS estimates by 3-5% over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 4,115 (versus INR 4,285 earlier). Maintain REDUCE.

UTI AMC | Reports loss due to other income; all is well<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21217&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 1,100

UTI AMC reported a loss of INR 514mn in 4Q only due to MTM losses resulting in negative other income of INR 1,630mn. Operating performance was sound; revenue fell just 1% QoQ while QAAUM was down 3%. Employee expenses were back to the trend rate of INR 1.3bn while other expenses were elevated with marketing efforts. In the concall, the CEO (who took over w.e.f. Feb’26) highlighted the focus on building an agile salesforce and a product suite that can deliver across market cycles to ultimately deliver “growth”. We believe we should look out in this quarter for the structural rejuvenation of India’s oldest AMC under new leadership and a vibrant team. We raise our TP slightly to INR 1,100 (from INR 1,050 earlier), valuing the AMC at 14x FY27E EPS of INR 78 (against 13x FY28E EPS of INR 82 earlier). We maintain ADD.

Indian Energy Exchange | 4QFY26 first cut: In-line; coupling overhang continues<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21216&DownloadType=show>

Flash Update

IEX reported in-line results, with trading volumes growing 24% YoY. FY26 market clearing prices in the DAM (-14% YoY) and RTM (-16% YoY) declined due to subdued power demand and increase in supply side liquidity. Key highlights from the 4QFY26 results are as follows- Revenue increased 23% YoY to INR 1.7 bn (-3% vs. JMFe / +3% consensus), driven by 24% YoY growth in trading volumes.- EBITDA margin reported at 85.7% vs. 85.3% / 86.1% in 4QFY25 / JMFe.- PAT came at INR 1.3 bn (11% YoY / -9% JMFe / -4% Cons), due to lower than expected other income (-32% YoY / -41% JMFe).- Market clearing prices in the DAM (Day-Ahead Market) and RTM (Real-Time Market) stood at INR 3.86 per unit and INR 3.59 per unit, down 14% and 16% YoY due to increased supply liquidity on the exchange.- The Board of Directors announced a final dividend of INR 2.0 per share.

Tips Music | Strong end to FY26; steady guidance for FY27<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21214&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 730

Tips Music reported strong 4QFY26 earnings growth of 92.9% YoY, driven by 32.4% revenue growth (19.1%/10.7%/21.4% in 1Q/2Q/3QFY26) and ~2,660bps EBIT margin expansion. Top-line growth was supported by sustained traction in the company’s catalogue, particularly its 90s repertoire, with management reiterating that growth was entirely recurring with no one-offs. Paid subscription contribution has inched up to 10–15% of digital revenue (10–12% last year). Margin expansion was largely led by lower content spends (down 54.6% YoY) due to deferment of a key movie-based music content release to 1QFY27. We broadly factor in management’s revenue guidance of ~20% revenue growth in FY27 (up from 15% expected earlier) but build content spends at the lower end of the guidance of INR 800mn-900mn basis historical under-delivery. Overall, we raise FY27E–28E EPS by 12–15%. We roll-forward to Mar’27 TP for a revised TP of INR 730 (INR 580 earlier) as we now value the stock at 32x P/E versus 30x earlier.

Bharat Electronics | Order momentum strong; valuations expensive<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21213&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     ADD                 INR 485

Bharat Electronics (BEL) is well placed to benefit from rising capex in India’s defence electronics. In our view, it could be a key beneficiary of electronic components, avionics and radar orders across large defence platforms. The company has secured order inflows of INR 300bn in FY26 (+60% YoY) ahead of its guidance of INR 270bn. We expect this ordering momentum to persist in FY27 too, with strong prospects of over INR 500bn. We forecast revenue/EPS CAGR of 16% each over FY26–28E. However, BEL’s valuation discount to capital goods growth stocks is close to an all-time low (discount at 8% versus 10-year average of 30%). In our view, the current stock price largely reflects these positives. We value BEL at 44x FY28 EPS of INR 11, arriving at our revised TP of INR 485 and assume coverage with an ADD rating.

Bharat Heavy Electricals | Journey of growth and profit begins<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21221&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     BUY                 INR 393

BHEL has run up 32% in just one month versus 20%/19% for S&P BSE Industrials/Power index. We believe order book has not yet peaked given 18/14GW of pipeline along with improving non-thermal visibility. With the commissioning of 8.9/10GW of legacy projects, pickup in both execution and margin is certain in our view. Moreover, we reckon the permission to import from China, change in product mix in non-thermal orders (HVDC, naval guns, nuclear), indigenisation of supercritical and op-lev would propel margins to mid-teens over next two–three years. Easing of working capital stress is also likely considering large customer advances and commissioning of legacy projects. The stock is trading at 27x FY28E EPS (consensus) at Q4FY26E EBITDA margin of 11% (consensus). We anticipate a beat on margins, making it worthy of an earnings upgrade and re-rating as revenues compound 20% over FY25–28E while EBITDA margin expands from 4.4% to 11.2% in base case and RoE improves to at least 15% by FY28E (versus 25–30% over 2008–12 cycle). These factors call for a premium in our view; accordingly, we value the stock at a P/E of 32x (up from 30x), yielding a revised TP of INR 393 (INR 345 earlier); reiterate ‘BUY’.

Company/Sector Update

Infosys | Soft 4QFY26; Healthy large deal wins<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21219&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 1,500

Infosys reported 4QFY26 results – revenue and margins were below expectations. Revenue declined 1.3% cc QoQ vs expectation of 0.5% cc QoQ decline. EBIT margins at 20.9% – missed JMFL and consensus expectations. Forward looking indicators – i) Infosys guided for 1.5%-3.5% YoY cc revenue growth for FY27 (includes 20-25bps of acquisition), lower than our expectation of 2% to 4%, margin guidance was in-line at 20-22%. ii) LTM large deal TCV grew ~28% YoY in FY26 versus decline of ~34% in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 0.74x in FY26 versus 0.60x in FY25). ii) Headcount declined 2.6% QoQ / up 1.6% YoY. iii) Management commentary – Lower end of guidance factors demand deterioration while top end factors demand improvement. Margin headwinds for FY27 also includes a potential 70bps dilutive impact from acquisitions. Prefer Infosys among the top 6 Indian IT companies given healthy large deal wins. Valuations are at ~16x FY27 consensus EPS. We revise our EPS estimates down by 1-2% over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 1,500 (versus INR 1525 earlier). Maintain BUY.

Tata Capital | Strong growth and asset quality trends; miss on NIM<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21220&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 380

Tata Capital reported a healthy quarter with PAT growth at +47% YoY/+16% QoQ (despite missing JMFe by 4.7%), and ~17bps/49bps increase in RoA/RoE at 2.1%/13%. This was driven by healthy AUM growth, improving asset quality and continued operating leverage. AUM grew +20% YoY / +6% QoQ (28% YoY ex-TMFL), in-line with their guidance, led by sustained traction in retail, corporate and housing finance. TCHFL remained a standout with +29% YoY AUM growth and best-in-class asset quality. However, at the overall level, NIM performance remained relatively softer than expected (-9bps QoQ, -15bps JMFe) as asset-side repricing and higher corporate mix continued to weigh on yields, leading to a miss in our PAT estimates. Credit cost improved sharply to 0.9% (0.8% ex-motor finance), while GS3/NS3 declined by 20bps/15bps QoQ. Motor finance also showed improving profitability. Management remains confident of delivering FY28 guidance of ~2.5–2.7% RoA (% of net loans) and >30% PAT CAGR. We believe improving earnings quality and motor finance normalisation support premium valuations. We build in EPS CAGR of ~34% during FY26-28E with average RoA (% of avg. assets)/RoE of ~2.2%/15% in FY27/28E (BAF/CIFC- EPS CAGR: ~28%/26%, RoA: ~4%/2.6%; RoE: ~21%/20% during the same period). We maintain ADD with revised TP of INR380, valuing it at ~2.7/18x FY28 BVPS/EPS (vs. ~3.8/19x for BAF/ ~3.1/18x for CIFC).

LTM | Soft 4QFY26; valuation remains elevated<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21218&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              REDUCE                       INR 4,115

LTM reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were below versus expectations. Revenue growth came in at 1.2% cc QoQ versus expectation of 1.6% cc QoQ. EBIT margins at 15.1% versus JMFL/consensus expectations of 15.6%/15.5%. Forward looking indicators – i) LTM order inflow grew 10.2% YoY in FY26 versus 7% YoY growth in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 1.4x in FY26 versus 1.3x in FY25). ii) Headcount remained stable on a sequential basis (+4.3% YoY). iii) Management commentary – confident to continue the growth momentum in FY27. However, due to certain macroeconomic environment that might happen within the three months period, there could be some softness in a particular quarter. Valuations are at ~21x FY27 consensus EPS. LTM is at ~7% premium to Mphasis for a similar growth profile in FY27, as per consensus. We reduce our EPS estimates by 3-5% over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 4,115 (versus INR 4,285 earlier). Maintain REDUCE.

UTI AMC | Reports loss due to other income; all is well<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21217&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 1,100

UTI AMC reported a loss of INR 514mn in 4Q only due to MTM losses resulting in negative other income of INR 1,630mn. Operating performance was sound; revenue fell just 1% QoQ while QAAUM was down 3%. Employee expenses were back to the trend rate of INR 1.3bn while other expenses were elevated with marketing efforts. In the concall, the CEO (who took over w.e.f. Feb’26) highlighted the focus on building an agile salesforce and a product suite that can deliver across market cycles to ultimately deliver “growth”. We believe we should look out in this quarter for the structural rejuvenation of India’s oldest AMC under new leadership and a vibrant team. We raise our TP slightly to INR 1,100 (from INR 1,050 earlier), valuing the AMC at 14x FY27E EPS of INR 78 (against 13x FY28E EPS of INR 82 earlier). We maintain ADD.

Indian Energy Exchange | 4QFY26 first cut: In-line; coupling overhang continues<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21216&DownloadType=show>

Flash Update

IEX reported in-line results, with trading volumes growing 24% YoY. FY26 market clearing prices in the DAM (-14% YoY) and RTM (-16% YoY) declined due to subdued power demand and increase in supply side liquidity. Key highlights from the 4QFY26 results are as follows- Revenue increased 23% YoY to INR 1.7 bn (-3% vs. JMFe / +3% consensus), driven by 24% YoY growth in trading volumes.- EBITDA margin reported at 85.7% vs. 85.3% / 86.1% in 4QFY25 / JMFe.- PAT came at INR 1.3 bn (11% YoY / -9% JMFe / -4% Cons), due to lower than expected other income (-32% YoY / -41% JMFe).- Market clearing prices in the DAM (Day-Ahead Market) and RTM (Real-Time Market) stood at INR 3.86 per unit and INR 3.59 per unit, down 14% and 16% YoY due to increased supply liquidity on the exchange.- The Board of Directors announced a final dividend of INR 2.0 per share.

Tips Music | Strong end to FY26; steady guidance for FY27<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21214&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 730

Tips Music reported strong 4QFY26 earnings growth of 92.9% YoY, driven by 32.4% revenue growth (19.1%/10.7%/21.4% in 1Q/2Q/3QFY26) and ~2,660bps EBIT margin expansion. Top-line growth was supported by sustained traction in the company’s catalogue, particularly its 90s repertoire, with management reiterating that growth was entirely recurring with no one-offs. Paid subscription contribution has inched up to 10–15% of digital revenue (10–12% last year). Margin expansion was largely led by lower content spends (down 54.6% YoY) due to deferment of a key movie-based music content release to 1QFY27. We broadly factor in management’s revenue guidance of ~20% revenue growth in FY27 (up from 15% expected earlier) but build content spends at the lower end of the guidance of INR 800mn-900mn basis historical under-delivery. Overall, we raise FY27E–28E EPS by 12–15%. We roll-forward to Mar’27 TP for a revised TP of INR 730 (INR 580 earlier) as we now value the stock at 32x P/E versus 30x earlier.

Bharat Electronics | Order momentum strong; valuations expensive<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21213&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     ADD                 INR 485

Bharat Electronics (BEL) is well placed to benefit from rising capex in India’s defence electronics. In our view, it could be a key beneficiary of electronic components, avionics and radar orders across large defence platforms. The company has secured order inflows of INR 300bn in FY26 (+60% YoY) ahead of its guidance of INR 270bn. We expect this ordering momentum to persist in FY27 too, with strong prospects of over INR 500bn. We forecast revenue/EPS CAGR of 16% each over FY26–28E. However, BEL’s valuation discount to capital goods growth stocks is close to an all-time low (discount at 8% versus 10-year average of 30%). In our view, the current stock price largely reflects these positives. We value BEL at 44x FY28 EPS of INR 11, arriving at our revised TP of INR 485 and assume coverage with an ADD rating.

Bharat Heavy Electricals | Journey of growth and profit begins<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21221&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     BUY                 INR 393

BHEL has run up 32% in just one month versus 20%/19% for S&P BSE Industrials/Power index. We believe order book has not yet peaked given 18/14GW of pipeline along with improving non-thermal visibility. With the commissioning of 8.9/10GW of legacy projects, pickup in both execution and margin is certain in our view. Moreover, we reckon the permission to import from China, change in product mix in non-thermal orders (HVDC, naval guns, nuclear), indigenisation of supercritical and op-lev would propel margins to mid-teens over next two–three years. Easing of working capital stress is also likely considering large customer advances and commissioning of legacy projects. The stock is trading at 27x FY28E EPS (consensus) at Q4FY26E EBITDA margin of 11% (consensus). We anticipate a beat on margins, making it worthy of an earnings upgrade and re-rating as revenues compound 20% over FY25–28E while EBITDA margin expands from 4.4% to 11.2% in base case and RoE improves to at least 15% by FY28E (versus 25–30% over 2008–12 cycle). These factors call for a premium in our view; accordingly, we value the stock at a P/E of 32x (up from 30x), yielding a revised TP of INR 393 (INR 345 earlier); reiterate ‘BUY’.

Havells India | 4Q a beat, but how sustainable is it?<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21211&DownloadType=show>

Rating Downgrade                   ADD                 INR 1,490

Havells missed 4Q revenue estimate but beat EBITDA/PAT estimates given (1) stronger C&W margin, and (2) lower-than-expected losses in Lloyd despite revenue declining 19% YoY. Within C&W, Havells reported ~6% volume growth, with wires (higher share of copper) volume declining while cables (mix of copper and aluminium) volume rose minimally; increasing price trends aided margin. The company indicated demand has improved off late in Lloyd after a weak start to the summer and inventory may normalise only by end-April. Further, within ECD, despite a YoY revenue decline of ~3%, commentary suggested no market share losses. Lastly, on margin, we note a medium-term aspiration to gain 200bps. That said, we build in a conservative 100bps expansion over FY26–29E given (1) Increased investments in A&P, brand visibility initiatives, and R&D, and (2) unlikelihood of C&W segment margin sustaining as there were gains from rising input costs in 4Q. We cut our FY27/28E estimates by ~3% and value Havells at 45x Mar’28E EPS, arriving at a TP of INR 1,490, implying ~10% upside to CMP. Hence, downgrade to ADD.

SBI Life Insurance | Best bet in an uncertain demand environment<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21208&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 2,200

SBI Life reported Q4FY26 VNB of INR 16.3bn, +2% JMFe, -2% YoY. Individual APE/total APE growth of 8%/6% YoY was below estimates, while margin surprised positively though it declined 210bps YoY to 28.3%. Management had expected a 1.74% full-year impact from GST 2.0 for this year, but it was lower at 1.50%. FY26 APE growth and margin were in line with guidance at 13%/27.5%. Management guided for 14% APE growth and 27–28% VNB margin for FY27E and the near term. We have hardly changed our VNB estimates; we slightly cut APE growth but raise margin forecasts. We maintain BUY with a revised TP of INR 2,200, valuing the stock at 1.9x Mar’28E EVPS of INR 1,158 (against 1.9x Mar’28E EVPS of INR 1,135 earlier). March industry volume data and these 4Q results reinforce our view that SBI Life is the best play in life insurance (a defensive sector in itself) when there is demand uncertainty; it remains our top pick in the space.

Tech Mahindra | Inline 4Q results; FY27 margin trajectory remains on track<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21209&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 1,555

Tech Mahindra reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were largely inline. Revenue growth came in at 0.6% cc QoQ vs expectation of 0.4% cc QoQ. EBIT margins at 13.8% – inline versus JMFL and consensus expectations. Forward looking indicators – i) LTM new TCV grew ~42% YoY in FY26 versus ~43% YoY new TCV growth in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 0.59x in FY26 versus 0.43x in FY25). ii) Headcount saw a slight decline -1.3% QoQ / -0.7% YoY. iii) Management commentary – confident of doing better than the industry growth and achieve 15% EBIT margins in FY27. Industry growth is expected to be 2-4% or 3-5%. Tech Mahindra has executed well on margins – already factored in the valuations, in our view. Valuations are at 19x FY27 consensus EPS, at c.7% premium to the sector. We revise our EPS estimates marginally over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 1,555 (versus INR 1,550 earlier). Maintain ADD.

Tata Communications | Earnings beat on strong digital portfolio growth<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21210&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 1,980

Tata Communications (TCOM) reported consolidated revenue of INR 65.5bn (up 5.9% QoQ and 9.4% YoY) for Q4FY26, higher than JMFe/consensus, led by strong growth across digital portfolio, core connectivity, voice segments and TCR. Data segment revenue was 4.2% higher than JMFe at INR 56.8bn (up 6.1% QoQ and 11.5% YoY) as digital portfolio revenue was 6.7% higher than JMFe at INR 29.1bn (up 9.4% QoQ and 19.2% YoY) and core connectivity revenue was also 1.7% higher at INR 27.7bn (up 2.8% QoQ and 4.5% YoY). However, consolidated EBITDA, at INR 12.8bn (up 4.5% QoQ and 14.4% YoY), was only 0.9-2.6% higher than JMFe/consensus due to higher network and other opex, resulting in EBITDA margin of 19.6% in Q4FY26 (19.8% in 3QFY26). Net debt fell QoQ to INR 94bn while capex fell QoQ to INR 5.4bn or 8.3% of revenue in 4Q. We maintain our BUY rating on TCOM (unchanged TP of INR 1,980/share) based on 10x FY28 EV/EBITDA multiple for the data segment (5Y historical average of 11x) as we expect data segment EBITDA to grow at a robust CAGR of ~20% over FY26–29E, driven by a) strong growth in digital portfolio, positioned strongly across key megatrends like cloud, AI, IoT, etc.; b) improvement in overall EBITDA margin from current ~20% to ~22% by FY28 (management guidance of 23–25%) as operating leverage and acquisition synergies are likely to be partly offset by adverse revenue mix.

Sunteck Realty | Strong pre-sales with improving collections<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21207&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 560

Sunteck Realty (SRL) posted robust FY26 pre-sales of c. INR 31.6bn (+25% YoY) with Q4FY26 at c. INR 10.6bn (+22% YoY; +45% QoQ; -4% JMFe), underpinned by sustained traction in premium and uber-luxury segments. Quarterly collections increased 39% YoY to c. INR 4.3bn; for FY26, collections rose 14% YoY to INR 14.3bn with an operating surplus of INR 5.5bn (+48% YoY). Collections are set to improve meaningfully over FY27/FY28 as execution picks up pace. Business development accelerated during the year with investments in land increasing to c. INR 8bn (from c. INR 1.8bn in FY25) and the addition of c. INR 50bn GDV across three new projects, with embedded margins of c. 30–35%. The launch pipeline remains healthy (c. INR 70bn GDV for FY27), including subsequent phases at Vasai and Naigaon alongside a recently signed JDA project in Mira Road. The Dubai project is launch-ready, subject to geopolitical normalisation. Overall, management expresses confidence in sustaining current pre-sales momentum into FY27, irrespective of the Dubai project launch. Pre-sales could expand at 27% CAGR over FY26–28E on the back of multiple launches. We reiterate BUY with a DCF based Mar’27E TP of INR 560.

PNC Infratech | Receives much awaited NHAI orders; backlog strengthens<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21204&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     BUY                 INR 345

PNC Infratech (PNC) has been declared L1 by the NHAI in two HAM road projects for 4-laning of the Barabanki–Biswariya stretch in Uttar Pradesh; this would have a total EPC value of c. INR 30bn. And given the packages are contiguous, we expect them to support margins. These wins are particularly positive as NHAI order inflows had remained slow over the past two years— for instance, PNC’s FY26 inflows aggregated INR 60bn versus guidance of INR 120bn. Now these fresh wins will strengthen order backlog and improve execution visibility. Order backlog stood at a strong INR 203bn (2.9x TTM revenue) at end-Dec-25. All in all, we reckon a robust EPS CAGR of 22% over FY25–28E. Meanwhile, the stock is trading at an attractive 10x/8x FY27E/28E EPS. All in all, our valuation and TP remain unchanged—we value the EPC business at 13x FY27E EPS yielding an SotP-based TP of INR 345; maintain BUY.

ABB India | Parent reported order inflows, which seems largely inline<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21206&DownloadType=show>

Flash Update

ABB India’s parent entity ABB Global has reported 1QCY26 numbers with overall 24% YoY growth in order inflows on a comparable basis (possibly adjusting for sale of Robotics & Automation). A positive read-through for India is the reported 1QCY26 order inflow of +26% YoY, the highest in the key AMEA region for ABB Global. This appears in-line with our estimate of INR43bn (preview note) of ~27% YoY order inflow (ex. Robotics) for ABB India in 1QCY26E. That said, margins may remain under pressure for ABB India. Moreover, current valuations make the risk reward profile significantly adverse, in our view.

Strategy | JM India Pulse – Mar’26<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21205&DownloadType=show>

Sector Update

In this note, we lay out the most important monthly market, sectoral and economic indicators tracked by the JM Financial research team. The indicators span high-frequency macro variables and sector-level operating metrics that help assess near-term growth momentum and emerging inflection points. Escalation in the West Asia conflict sent Brent crude oil price soaring past the USD 100/bbl mark. FII selling intensified and the INR weakened; however, the trade deficit narrowed in Mar’26. The domestic environment reflects selective strength led by improving discretionary consumption and credit growth amid easing financial conditions. Household financialisation remains resilient even as digital payment growth normalises. Industrial activity moderated slightly with capex remaining uneven. Rising oil and gas prices, supply disruptions and geopolitical uncertainty have driven the recent volatility in Indian equity markets, though peace talks between the US and Iran have improved market sentiment.

Best regards

(Priyankar Biswas, priyankar.biswas@jmfl.com, Tel: (91 22) 69703622)

•      Strategy | JM India Pulse – Mar’26<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21205&DownloadType=show>

(Venkatesh Balasubramaniam, venkatesh.balasubramaniam@jmfl.com, Tel: (91 22) 66303081)

Havells India | 4Q a beat, but how sustainable is it?<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21211&DownloadType=show>

Rating Downgrade                   ADD                 INR 1,490

Havells missed 4Q revenue estimate but beat EBITDA/PAT estimates given (1) stronger C&W margin, and (2) lower-than-expected losses in Lloyd despite revenue declining 19% YoY. Within C&W, Havells reported ~6% volume growth, with wires (higher share of copper) volume declining while cables (mix of copper and aluminium) volume rose minimally; increasing price trends aided margin. The company indicated demand has improved off late in Lloyd after a weak start to the summer and inventory may normalise only by end-April. Further, within ECD, despite a YoY revenue decline of ~3%, commentary suggested no market share losses. Lastly, on margin, we note a medium-term aspiration to gain 200bps. That said, we build in a conservative 100bps expansion over FY26–29E given (1) Increased investments in A&P, brand visibility initiatives, and R&D, and (2) unlikelihood of C&W segment margin sustaining as there were gains from rising input costs in 4Q. We cut our FY27/28E estimates by ~3% and value Havells at 45x Mar’28E EPS, arriving at a TP of INR 1,490, implying ~10% upside to CMP. Hence, downgrade to ADD.

SBI Life Insurance | Best bet in an uncertain demand environment<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21208&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 2,200

SBI Life reported Q4FY26 VNB of INR 16.3bn, +2% JMFe, -2% YoY. Individual APE/total APE growth of 8%/6% YoY was below estimates, while margin surprised positively though it declined 210bps YoY to 28.3%. Management had expected a 1.74% full-year impact from GST 2.0 for this year, but it was lower at 1.50%. FY26 APE growth and margin were in line with guidance at 13%/27.5%. Management guided for 14% APE growth and 27–28% VNB margin for FY27E and the near term. We have hardly changed our VNB estimates; we slightly cut APE growth but raise margin forecasts. We maintain BUY with a revised TP of INR 2,200, valuing the stock at 1.9x Mar’28E EVPS of INR 1,158 (against 1.9x Mar’28E EVPS of INR 1,135 earlier). March industry volume data and these 4Q results reinforce our view that SBI Life is the best play in life insurance (a defensive sector in itself) when there is demand uncertainty; it remains our top pick in the space.

Tech Mahindra | Inline 4Q results; FY27 margin trajectory remains on track<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21209&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 1,555

Tech Mahindra reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were largely inline. Revenue growth came in at 0.6% cc QoQ vs expectation of 0.4% cc QoQ. EBIT margins at 13.8% – inline versus JMFL and consensus expectations. Forward looking indicators – i) LTM new TCV grew ~42% YoY in FY26 versus ~43% YoY new TCV growth in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 0.59x in FY26 versus 0.43x in FY25). ii) Headcount saw a slight decline -1.3% QoQ / -0.7% YoY. iii) Management commentary – confident of doing better than the industry growth and achieve 15% EBIT margins in FY27. Industry growth is expected to be 2-4% or 3-5%. Tech Mahindra has executed well on margins – already factored in the valuations, in our view. Valuations are at 19x FY27 consensus EPS, at c.7% premium to the sector. We revise our EPS estimates marginally over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 1,555 (versus INR 1,550 earlier). Maintain ADD.

Tata Communications | Earnings beat on strong digital portfolio growth<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21210&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 1,980

Tata Communications (TCOM) reported consolidated revenue of INR 65.5bn (up 5.9% QoQ and 9.4% YoY) for Q4FY26, higher than JMFe/consensus, led by strong growth across digital portfolio, core connectivity, voice segments and TCR. Data segment revenue was 4.2% higher than JMFe at INR 56.8bn (up 6.1% QoQ and 11.5% YoY) as digital portfolio revenue was 6.7% higher than JMFe at INR 29.1bn (up 9.4% QoQ and 19.2% YoY) and core connectivity revenue was also 1.7% higher at INR 27.7bn (up 2.8% QoQ and 4.5% YoY). However, consolidated EBITDA, at INR 12.8bn (up 4.5% QoQ and 14.4% YoY), was only 0.9-2.6% higher than JMFe/consensus due to higher network and other opex, resulting in EBITDA margin of 19.6% in Q4FY26 (19.8% in 3QFY26). Net debt fell QoQ to INR 94bn while capex fell QoQ to INR 5.4bn or 8.3% of revenue in 4Q. We maintain our BUY rating on TCOM (unchanged TP of INR 1,980/share) based on 10x FY28 EV/EBITDA multiple for the data segment (5Y historical average of 11x) as we expect data segment EBITDA to grow at a robust CAGR of ~20% over FY26–29E, driven by a) strong growth in digital portfolio, positioned strongly across key megatrends like cloud, AI, IoT, etc.; b) improvement in overall EBITDA margin from current ~20% to ~22% by FY28 (management guidance of 23–25%) as operating leverage and acquisition synergies are likely to be partly offset by adverse revenue mix.

Sunteck Realty | Strong pre-sales with improving collections<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21207&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              BUY                 INR 560

Sunteck Realty (SRL) posted robust FY26 pre-sales of c. INR 31.6bn (+25% YoY) with Q4FY26 at c. INR 10.6bn (+22% YoY; +45% QoQ; -4% JMFe), underpinned by sustained traction in premium and uber-luxury segments. Quarterly collections increased 39% YoY to c. INR 4.3bn; for FY26, collections rose 14% YoY to INR 14.3bn with an operating surplus of INR 5.5bn (+48% YoY). Collections are set to improve meaningfully over FY27/FY28 as execution picks up pace. Business development accelerated during the year with investments in land increasing to c. INR 8bn (from c. INR 1.8bn in FY25) and the addition of c. INR 50bn GDV across three new projects, with embedded margins of c. 30–35%. The launch pipeline remains healthy (c. INR 70bn GDV for FY27), including subsequent phases at Vasai and Naigaon alongside a recently signed JDA project in Mira Road. The Dubai project is launch-ready, subject to geopolitical normalisation. Overall, management expresses confidence in sustaining current pre-sales momentum into FY27, irrespective of the Dubai project launch. Pre-sales could expand at 27% CAGR over FY26–28E on the back of multiple launches. We reiterate BUY with a DCF based Mar’27E TP of INR 560.

PNC Infratech | Receives much awaited NHAI orders; backlog strengthens<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21204&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     BUY                 INR 345

PNC Infratech (PNC) has been declared L1 by the NHAI in two HAM road projects for 4-laning of the Barabanki–Biswariya stretch in Uttar Pradesh; this would have a total EPC value of c. INR 30bn. And given the packages are contiguous, we expect them to support margins. These wins are particularly positive as NHAI order inflows had remained slow over the past two years— for instance, PNC’s FY26 inflows aggregated INR 60bn versus guidance of INR 120bn. Now these fresh wins will strengthen order backlog and improve execution visibility. Order backlog stood at a strong INR 203bn (2.9x TTM revenue) at end-Dec-25. All in all, we reckon a robust EPS CAGR of 22% over FY25–28E. Meanwhile, the stock is trading at an attractive 10x/8x FY27E/28E EPS. All in all, our valuation and TP remain unchanged—we value the EPC business at 13x FY27E EPS yielding an SotP-based TP of INR 345; maintain BUY.

ABB India | Parent reported order inflows, which seems largely inline<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21206&DownloadType=show>

Flash Update

ABB India’s parent entity ABB Global has reported 1QCY26 numbers with overall 24% YoY growth in order inflows on a comparable basis (possibly adjusting for sale of Robotics & Automation). A positive read-through for India is the reported 1QCY26 order inflow of +26% YoY, the highest in the key AMEA region for ABB Global. This appears in-line with our estimate of INR43bn (preview note) of ~27% YoY order inflow (ex. Robotics) for ABB India in 1QCY26E. That said, margins may remain under pressure for ABB India. Moreover, current valuations make the risk reward profile significantly adverse, in our view.

Strategy | JM India Pulse – Mar’26<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21205&DownloadType=show>

Sector Update

In this note, we lay out the most important monthly market, sectoral and economic indicators tracked by the JM Financial research team. The indicators span high-frequency macro variables and sector-level operating metrics that help assess near-term growth momentum and emerging inflection points. Escalation in the West Asia conflict sent Brent crude oil price soaring past the USD 100/bbl mark. FII selling intensified and the INR weakened; however, the trade deficit narrowed in Mar’26. The domestic environment reflects selective strength led by improving discretionary consumption and credit growth amid easing financial conditions. Household financialisation remains resilient even as digital payment growth normalises. Industrial activity moderated slightly with capex remaining uneven. Rising oil and gas prices, supply disruptions and geopolitical uncertainty have driven the recent volatility in Indian equity markets, though peace talks between the US and Iran have improved market sentiment.

Best regards
JM Financial Research

*Subject:* JMFS Fundamental Research - Daily Financial Market Digest (24th

*Dear All,*

*JMFS Research *

*April 24th, 2026*

*Daily Financial Market Digest*

*Top Corporate News & sector developments*

*Lux Industries:* Company's board has given in-principle approval to
demerge the business into three distinct verticals (with Verticals A and C
being listed separately) while the PKT family retains overall control
following a family settlement that also revises brand licensing pacts.
*Positive*

*Inventurus Knowledge Solutions*: The company has announced a major
cross-border acquisition, signing a deal to buy US-based healthcare IT firm
TruBridge for up to $565 million. *Positive*

*Himadri Speciality Chemical:* The company has successfully commenced
operations at its new anode material production facility in West Bengal,
which boasts an installed capacity of 200 MTPA. *Positive*

*Gujarat Themis Biosyn:* Company has signed an Asset Purchase Agreement
with Sanofi to acquire 13 established branded generic products for €158
million in cash, expected to close by December 2026. The portfolio, present
in 55+ countries across Europe, the Middle East and Africa, reported net
sales of ~€62 million in 2025. It strengthens GTBL's global generics
platform, expands its anti-infective presence and enables forward
integration leveraging its existing API and fermentation capabilities.

*Eternal:* As per media reports Zomato has agreed to drop a contract term
that penalized restaurants for offering cheaper meals to walk-in diners,
after opposition from eateries who said the policy undermined their pricing
decisions. The clause was never enforced but has been dropped now.

*Force Motors:* The automaker has executed an agreement to acquire Veera
Tanneries for Rs 162 crore, turning it into a wholly-owned subsidiary.
*Positive*

*Waaree energies & Vikram Solar:* The US Commerce Department has imposed
preliminary anti-dumping duties on solar imports from India (123%), Laos
(22.46%), and Indonesia (35.17%), following a petition by American solar
manufacturers alleging unfair pricing. Combined with existing
countervailing duties, total AD/CVD rates reach ~234% for India. *Negative*

*GMR Airports:* ADP (Paris airport operator) plans to sell up to a 7.3%
stake in GMR Airports for ~€924 million ($1.08 billion). The deal begins
with an immediate offload of a 3.4% stake for €256 million (~₹2,800 crore),
with the remainder to follow.

*4QFY26 Business Update*

*United Breweries:* Heineken India saw net revenue grow low-single-digit.
Total volume (including contract brewing now classified as licensed volume)
grew mid-single-digit. Premium segment grew mid-teens, led by Kingfisher
Ultra. Launched Kingfisher Smooth in mainstream segment.

*Gujarat Gas:* The company ensured an uninterrupted gas supply to the Morbi
unit to support the revival of the local ceramic industry, providing price
stability amid ongoing geopolitical disruptions that previously caused a
surge in raw material costs. Gas consumption in Morbi has rebounded to
~2.70 Mmscmd in April after operations of several ceramic units were
suspended in March, which had temporarily affected the livelihood of nearly
2 lakh workers.

*Aditya Birla Real Estate (FY26):* Birla Estates reported booking value of
Rs 8,136 crore. Collections increased 23.5% year-on-year.

*4QFY26 Result Update*

*Tata Capital:* Company reported a healthy quarter with PAT growth at +47%
YoY/+16% QoQ (despite missing estimate by 4.7%), and ~17bps/49bps increase
in RoA/RoE at 2.1%/13%. This was driven by healthy AUM growth, improving
asset quality and continued operating leverage. AUM grew +20% YoY / +6% QoQ
(28% YoY ex-TMFL), in-line with their guidance, led by sustained traction
in retail, corporate and housing finance. TCHFL remained a standout with
+29% YoY AUM growth and best-in-class asset quality. However, at the
overall level, NIM performance remained relatively softer than expected
(-9bps QoQ, -15bps JMFe) as asset-side repricing and higher corporate mix
continued to weigh on yields, leading to a miss in our PAT estimates.
Credit cost improved sharply to 0.9% (0.8% ex-motor finance), while GS3/NS3
declined by 20bps/15bps QoQ. *Strong Results*

*Infosys: *Company reported 4QFY26 results – revenue and margins were below
expectations. Revenues came in at USD 5,040mn, -1.2% QoQ/+6.6% YoY
(estimates: USD 5,101mn). Revenues declined 1.3% cc QoQ vs estimates of
-0.5% QoQ.  EBIT margin came in at 20.9%, down ~20bps QoQ, vs estimates of
21.2%. Margin tailwinds: currency (+40bps), realisations (+30bps), Margin
headwinds: amortization impact (-50bps), one-off gain normalization
(-30bps), employee costs (-20 bps). Reported PAT stood at Rs 85.1bn, versus
JMFL estimates of Rs 77.7bn, PAT was supported by reversal of tax of Rs
7.7bn. Infy adr was down 4%. *Weak Results*

*LTM:* Company reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were below
versus expectations. Revenues came in at USD 1,222mn, +1.2% QoQ/+8.1% YoY
(estimates: USD 1,227mn). Revenues grew 1.2% cc QoQ vs estimates of 1.6%
QoQ. EBIT margin came in at 15.1%, down 100bps QoQ/ up 130bps YoY, vs
estimates of 15.6%. This was largely due to partial wage hikes and
productivity commitments in key accounts, partially offset by FX gains.
Reported PAT stood at Rs 13.9bn, versus estimates of Rs 14.8bn. PAT was
positively impacted by reversal of Rs 622mn in labour code impact. *Weak
Results*

*Cyient:* Company reported mixed Q4 results, with revenue increasing 4.2%
QoQ to ₹1,927 crore from ₹1,849 crore. However, profitability weakened as
EBIT declined 6.8% QoQ to ₹156 crore from ₹167 crore, with EBIT margin
contracting by 95 basis points to 8.08% from 9.03%. Net profit saw a sharp
drop of 40.3% QoQ, falling to ₹54.8 crore compared to ₹91.8 crore in the
previous quarter.

*CIE Automotive India:* Company reported a strong performance in Q4FY26,
with revenue rising to Rs 2611.9 crore from Rs 2272.6 crore, marking a
14.9% YoY and 9.1% QoQ increase. EBITDA grew to Rs 401.9 crore from Rs
335.5 crore, up 19.8% YoY and 20.1% QoQ, while EBITDA margin improved to
15.4% compared to 14.7% YoY and 13.9% QoQ. Profit after tax also saw solid
growth, reaching Rs 249.3 crore versus Rs 206.4 crore, reflecting a 20.8%
YoY and 22.0% QoQ increase.

*Corporate Action*

*Cyient Ltd:* The company has announced a share buyback worth Rs 720 crore.
The buyback will be executed at a fixed price of Rs 1,125 per share, which
reflects a premium of nearly 20 per cent over the stock’s closing price.
The company will carry out the entire process through the tender offer route

*Rolex Rings:* The company's Board has approved a buyback of up to 1 crore
equity shares at ₹180 per share, totalling ₹180 crores. The shares, with a
face value of ₹1 each, represent 3.67% of the existing paid-up equity
capital. The buyback will be conducted via the tender offer route, open to
all equity shareholders on a proportionate basis as of a record date to be
announced.

Forward looking indicators – i) Infosys guided for 1.5%-3.5% YoY cc revenue growth for FY27 (includes 20-25bps of acquisition), lower than our expectation of 2% to 4%, margin guidance was in-line at 20-22%. ii) LTM large deal TCV grew ~28% YoY in FY26 versus decline of ~34% in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 0.74x in FY26 versus 0.60x in FY25). ii) Headcount declined 2.6% QoQ / up 1.6% YoY. iii) Management commentary – Lower end of guidance factors demand deterioration while top end factors demand improvement. Margin headwinds for FY27 also includes a potential 70bps dilutive impact from acquisitions. Prefer Infosys among the top 6 Indian IT companies given healthy large deal wins. Valuations are at ~16x FY27 consensus EPS. We revise our EPS estimates down by 1-2% over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 1,500 (versus INR 1525 earlier). Maintain BUY.

Tata Capital | Strong growth and asset quality trends; miss on NIM<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21220&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 380

Tata Capital reported a healthy quarter with PAT growth at +47% YoY/+16% QoQ (despite missing JMFe by 4.7%), and ~17bps/49bps increase in RoA/RoE at 2.1%/13%. This was driven by healthy AUM growth, improving asset quality and continued operating leverage. AUM grew +20% YoY / +6% QoQ (28% YoY ex-TMFL), in-line with their guidance, led by sustained traction in retail, corporate and housing finance. TCHFL remained a standout with +29% YoY AUM growth and best-in-class asset quality. However, at the overall level, NIM performance remained relatively softer than expected (-9bps QoQ, -15bps JMFe) as asset-side repricing and higher corporate mix continued to weigh on yields, leading to a miss in our PAT estimates. Credit cost improved sharply to 0.9% (0.8% ex-motor finance), while GS3/NS3 declined by 20bps/15bps QoQ. Motor finance also showed improving profitability. Management remains confident of delivering FY28 guidance of ~2.5–2.7% RoA (% of net loans) and >30% PAT CAGR. We believe improving earnings quality and motor finance normalisation support premium valuations. We build in EPS CAGR of ~34% during FY26-28E with average RoA (% of avg. assets)/RoE of ~2.2%/15% in FY27/28E (BAF/CIFC- EPS CAGR: ~28%/26%, RoA: ~4%/2.6%; RoE: ~21%/20% during the same period). We maintain ADD with revised TP of INR380, valuing it at ~2.7/18x FY28 BVPS/EPS (vs. ~3.8/19x for BAF/ ~3.1/18x for CIFC).

LTM | Soft 4QFY26; valuation remains elevated<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21218&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              REDUCE                       INR 4,115

LTM reported 4QFY26 results – revenues and margins were below versus expectations. Revenue growth came in at 1.2% cc QoQ versus expectation of 1.6% cc QoQ. EBIT margins at 15.1% versus JMFL/consensus expectations of 15.6%/15.5%. Forward looking indicators – i) LTM order inflow grew 10.2% YoY in FY26 versus 7% YoY growth in FY25 (LTM book to bill of 1.4x in FY26 versus 1.3x in FY25). ii) Headcount remained stable on a sequential basis (+4.3% YoY). iii) Management commentary – confident to continue the growth momentum in FY27. However, due to certain macroeconomic environment that might happen within the three months period, there could be some softness in a particular quarter. Valuations are at ~21x FY27 consensus EPS. LTM is at ~7% premium to Mphasis for a similar growth profile in FY27, as per consensus. We reduce our EPS estimates by 3-5% over FY27-28E, incorporating 4Q results. Maintain target multiple of 19x Sep 27 – revised TP of INR 4,115 (versus INR 4,285 earlier). Maintain REDUCE.

UTI AMC | Reports loss due to other income; all is well<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21217&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 1,100

UTI AMC reported a loss of INR 514mn in 4Q only due to MTM losses resulting in negative other income of INR 1,630mn. Operating performance was sound; revenue fell just 1% QoQ while QAAUM was down 3%. Employee expenses were back to the trend rate of INR 1.3bn while other expenses were elevated with marketing efforts. In the concall, the CEO (who took over w.e.f. Feb’26) highlighted the focus on building an agile salesforce and a product suite that can deliver across market cycles to ultimately deliver “growth”. We believe we should look out in this quarter for the structural rejuvenation of India’s oldest AMC under new leadership and a vibrant team. We raise our TP slightly to INR 1,100 (from INR 1,050 earlier), valuing the AMC at 14x FY27E EPS of INR 78 (against 13x FY28E EPS of INR 82 earlier). We maintain ADD.

Indian Energy Exchange | 4QFY26 first cut: In-line; coupling overhang continues<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21216&DownloadType=show>

Flash Update

IEX reported in-line results, with trading volumes growing 24% YoY. FY26 market clearing prices in the DAM (-14% YoY) and RTM (-16% YoY) declined due to subdued power demand and increase in supply side liquidity. Key highlights from the 4QFY26 results are as follows- Revenue increased 23% YoY to INR 1.7 bn (-3% vs. JMFe / +3% consensus), driven by 24% YoY growth in trading volumes.- EBITDA margin reported at 85.7% vs. 85.3% / 86.1% in 4QFY25 / JMFe.- PAT came at INR 1.3 bn (11% YoY / -9% JMFe / -4% Cons), due to lower than expected other income (-32% YoY / -41% JMFe).- Market clearing prices in the DAM (Day-Ahead Market) and RTM (Real-Time Market) stood at INR 3.86 per unit and INR 3.59 per unit, down 14% and 16% YoY due to increased supply liquidity on the exchange.- The Board of Directors announced a final dividend of INR 2.0 per share.

Tips Music | Strong end to FY26; steady guidance for FY27<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21214&DownloadType=show>

Result Update              ADD                 INR 730

Tips Music reported strong 4QFY26 earnings growth of 92.9% YoY, driven by 32.4% revenue growth (19.1%/10.7%/21.4% in 1Q/2Q/3QFY26) and ~2,660bps EBIT margin expansion. Top-line growth was supported by sustained traction in the company’s catalogue, particularly its 90s repertoire, with management reiterating that growth was entirely recurring with no one-offs. Paid subscription contribution has inched up to 10–15% of digital revenue (10–12% last year). Margin expansion was largely led by lower content spends (down 54.6% YoY) due to deferment of a key movie-based music content release to 1QFY27. We broadly factor in management’s revenue guidance of ~20% revenue growth in FY27 (up from 15% expected earlier) but build content spends at the lower end of the guidance of INR 800mn-900mn basis historical under-delivery. Overall, we raise FY27E–28E EPS by 12–15%. We roll-forward to Mar’27 TP for a revised TP of INR 730 (INR 580 earlier) as we now value the stock at 32x P/E versus 30x earlier.

Bharat Electronics | Order momentum strong; valuations expensive<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21213&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     ADD                 INR 485

Bharat Electronics (BEL) is well placed to benefit from rising capex in India’s defence electronics. In our view, it could be a key beneficiary of electronic components, avionics and radar orders across large defence platforms. The company has secured order inflows of INR 300bn in FY26 (+60% YoY) ahead of its guidance of INR 270bn. We expect this ordering momentum to persist in FY27 too, with strong prospects of over INR 500bn. We forecast revenue/EPS CAGR of 16% each over FY26–28E. However, BEL’s valuation discount to capital goods growth stocks is close to an all-time low (discount at 8% versus 10-year average of 30%). In our view, the current stock price largely reflects these positives. We value BEL at 44x FY28 EPS of INR 11, arriving at our revised TP of INR 485 and assume coverage with an ADD rating.

Bharat Heavy Electricals | Journey of growth and profit begins<https://jmflresearch.com/Download?ReportId=21221&DownloadType=show>

Company Update                     BUY                 INR 393

BHEL has run up 32% in just one month versus 20%/19% for S&P BSE Industrials/Power index. We believe order book has not yet peaked given 18/14GW of pipeline along with improving non-thermal visibility. With the commissioning of 8.9/10GW of legacy projects, pickup in both execution and margin is certain in our view. Moreover, we reckon the permission to import from China, change in product mix in non-thermal orders (HVDC, naval guns, nuclear), indigenisation of supercritical and op-lev would propel margins to mid-teens over next two–three years. Easing of working capital stress is also likely considering large customer advances and commissioning of legacy projects. The stock is trading at 27x FY28E EPS (consensus) at Q4FY26E EBITDA margin of 11% (consensus). We anticipate a beat on margins, making it worthy of an earnings upgrade and re-rating as revenues compound 20% over FY25–28E while EBITDA margin expands from 4.4% to 11.2% in base case and RoE improves to at least 15% by FY28E (versus 25–30% over 2008–12 cycle). These factors call for a premium in our view; accordingly, we value the stock at a P/E of 32x (up from 30x), yielding a revised TP of INR 393 (INR 345 earlier); reiterate ‘BUY’.
